title: Multi-step onboarding wizard with push_patch URL state
prompt:
Write lib/my_app_web/live/onboarding_wizard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.OnboardingWizardLive). The wizard has 3 steps: profile, preferences, review. Use handle_params/3 to drive step transitions via the URL (query string ?step=profile|preferences|review). Each handle_event("next", _, socket) and handle_event("back", _, socket) must use push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/onboarding?step=#{new_step}") — NOT push_navigate (same LiveView) and NOT redirect. Use a single <.form> with to_form/2 that lives across all three steps; show different <.input> fields per step via :if. Browser back/forward must work correctly because the URL drives the state. Render the current step title with

Profile

, etc.
tier_rationale: Requires orchestrating 4 capabilities: push_patch-vs-push_navigate decision, handle_params-driven state, :if conditional rendering, and to_form lifecycle across step transitions. Sonnet typically picks push_navigate (which re-mounts) or breaks the form state across steps.
research_citation: georgeguimaraes phoenix-thinking: 'push_patch for same-LiveView URL updates; push_navigate re-mounts'
